review_title: If you don't need integration with an external app, this is the best time tracker I've seen
description: Response time of MyHours is amazing. You click, it starts. Some other software are simply too slow to process information or start tasks and I'm always wondering if the task timer has started or not. Also, this UI actually incites you to take notes which is never something you do naturally because, at that moment, you KNOW what you are working on. However, two months down the line, when the client says "what is this 4h30 log there ??", you can respond clearly "Oh you called me and you procrastinated for hours before making a decision, that's why it took so long dude !" :))
author: Nadege B.
pros: - Its user interface is pretty, fast-reacting, intuitive. It takes no time to understand how it works. - You can assign the same task to various projects, which is pretty cool : the fact that I have several projects where the same tasks apply (I manage a client's clients and for each of them, I have translations, website audit, profile analysis to do... So it's great to create the task once and for all and assign it to each sub-client). - With the invoicing feature, you can see immediately what's been invoiced, what's to be invoiced. As a freelancer, this is great as you don't necessarily invoice all your clients at the end of a month. Some yes, but some are invoiced at the end of a project. No looking around, exports, calculations, to see who needs to be invoiced. - Because of how it's set up, unless you are really not focused, it's pretty impossible to have logs that don't correspond to a project and a client. My previous time tracking softwares were leaving me with unidentified logs that kept me wondering for hours on what project / task I worked three weeks ago, at 1.21am !! :) I ended up deleting logs because I had no clue if it was a mistake or a real work log... - You can set up hourly rates per task or per project or per team member. That comes in handy because, in my case, I have different clients that I charge differently. And within the same client, I also have different rates depending on the project. - Pricing is spot on.
cons: Liking "least" is not how I would define it... I would say there is room for improvement in a couple of areas that are, right now, not critical for me (but might be for others): - No integration (that I know of) with productivity software / project management software (Asana, Basecamp, Monday, Slack, Airtable, etc...). For some, it will prove a bit wasteful to have tasks in those apps and to have to repeat them in MyHours. - Filtering is limited. For example, I have two clients that are close partners and even though I invoice them separately, projects are intertwined. I need to group those two clients together when I report and analyze time spent on projects. Right now, I can only filter one client at a time and I would appreciate filter where you can multiple select. - Like all time trackers I know, MyHours also doesn't provide a really great to have feature : providing a read only view for clients of logs applying to them. For example, one of my clients for whom I work on a daily basis needs to know my "count" for the month at any given time, so he knows if he can give me more work or not, since we are on an hours package basis... Having a permanent URL where he can check on his own where I'm at and what I've worked on and how long would avoid time wasted for him asking me and time wasted for me telling him :)
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Streamlined Time Tracking and Billing
description: I use this software daily (and all day, at that) both at home and when I'm working from the road. I cannot recommend it enough for tracking where I spend my time for my clients and what I need to invoice them at the end of each month.
author: Sabrina G.
pros: MyHours was the first system I found that let me really customize my solution the way that I wanted and needed it for my own tracking. I love the fact that I can have clients with different rates, team members with different rates, multiple projects for clients, and tasks under those projects. It's incredible the amount of customization on such an easy-to-navigate product.
cons: In some ways, it has too many features for my small business and it took me a bit longer to set up my own preferred flow of clients / projects / tasks. But, huge caveat, when I set it up, I was only testing it out and didn't think about future use. If I had planned out my implementation with long-term use in mind, it probably would not have taken me nearly as long to do this part.

review_title: Great lightweight utility for tracking time
description: As a small business with only a handful of clients, it's vitally important for me to know how much time I'm actually spending on a project. I've tried various time-tracking tools and My Hours is, by far, the best value of the bunch: Just the right amount of functionality without too many complications, and at a fair cost. I've recommended it to others on several occasions.
author: Bob R.
pros: My Hours is an affordable, simple system to track time for anyone or any organization that works by the hour. I started with it as an individual and it was a great tool for helping me improve my estimates and increase profitability of the work I took on. I've since expanded it to a small team, and it's my main resource for time management and substantiating client invoices.
cons: There are a few issues, such as the selector window shuts down when you reach the bottom of a list - so if you're just a little bit heavy on the mouse and miss the right moment to click, you end up having to go back and re-select the client/project etc. Also, a recent change in the way reports work has forced me to change the way I do billing. I find it frustrating but no more so than the unanticipated changes every online service provider makes from time to time.

review_title: Great time tracker!
author: Jaimie H.
pros: I love the ease-of-use with the time tracker and the ability to review all time tracked each week. It helps me easily put together my invoices at the end of each month.
cons: The Reports dashboard is a little confusing and could be improved.

review_title: Out of competition
description: Good, don't want to change it.
author: Roeland S.
pros: We are using My Hours for more than 5-6 years now. Used the paid and the free version. Both do compell exactly to our expections. You want to invoice a customer per project, per hour, per period, as consultancy, per... you can choose. Reporting is easy and accurate.
cons: Sometimes total hours versus decimal results are not 1 on 1 but you can't make an egg square also...

review_title: MyHours - great way to track my time
description: Great way to help me prepare my invoices for clients
author: Kim M.
pros: It is really easy to set up. Day to day it works great! And I love that it is free - for 1 person and not a team to use, the free version makes the most sense.
cons: They often upgrade things and then I have to figure out what the changes are Sometimes the changes are not for the best.

review_title: Best Hourly Software for Virtual Assistants
description: I often work on projects that are paid by the hour and I use My Hours to time myself as I work. It's so easy to customize to each project!
author: Stephanie L. W.
pros: With a virtual career, I often need to track my hours when working. I have been through several different types of time management software and only My Hours has worked for my needs. It's customizable for my business and its interface works seamlessly between all my pieces of technology. I really like how easy it is to pull reports from the Dashboard when I need to submit my hours to the company.
cons: There is only one feature missing from the software that I have determined after using it for almost a year. When a report is pulled up, I would like to be able to click to a certain day on the graph. Right now the bar graphs are not hyperlinked to the actual working hours.

review_title: Keeping track of working hours has never been so easy!
description: The user experience is great! Simple to use, delivers what it offers. The freemium is great and for many more than enough. Also good to organise yoour time and keep track of what you've been doing during the week. Good to follow up with the customers and say this is what's been done this week/month.
author: German U.
pros: The fact that you can create customers, activities and tags that then you can easily filter on the analytics works very well for me. Also the Week view where you can see all the activities and also the stopwatch option to track activities on the go.
cons: Haven't found one yet to be honest. Some times when starting a new week in the view week, I have to reorganise all the activities from previous weeks (I can copy them but then cannot modify without modifying the previous weeks).

review_title: Easy to Use & Customize
description: I looked at several free apps and tried a few. This one was so easy to get up and running. And to customize to suit your needs is a plus. Free version is just right for my needs.
author: Cecily M.
pros: Easy to set up to work the way you need it. I wanted to switch to military time and this was available. The tasks feature works well if needed. Great you have the ability to see on daily view or weekly. this helps when filling in my timesheet at the end of the week.
cons: I accidentally deleted a couple days of hours and couldn't find a way to recover. I'm using their free version so maybe available in their paid version.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easy to use, with a free option
description: I appreciate that there is a free option for me to use for the limited needs that I currently have. If I need pro features in the future, it would make sense to continue using My Hours. I love that it is easy to use and that that phone/computer apps are available
author: Jacqui S.
pros: I like that it is easy to use and that there is a free option.
cons: Thanks to having a free option to be able to use My Hours for my current needs, I have no cons.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Time will tell.
description: I use MyHours every day. It keeps me sane. It stops we overworking or underworking. It also gives me the power to assess individual areas of my business, life, and projects so I can fine tune and not live in la la land when it comes to understanding how long creative projects (and non-creative for that matter) actually take. Critical when it comes to pricing projects and determining the right fee level.
author: Richard C.
pros: You can copy from the previous week so you don't have to rebuild your timesheet. You can see your whole week spread of projects, tasks, and time and edit the whole lot with an overview of everything.
cons: That it didn't have this until recently! You can see your whole week spread of projects, tasks, and time and edit the whole lot with an overview of everything.

review_title: easy to use
author: Melissa D.
pros: I like using My Hours because it has a simple interface that gives me all the info I need for my solopreneur business.
cons: I have no complaints with the product. The free version gives me all the functionality that I need.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: best option
author: Peter P.
pros: all the necessary functions, while keeping it simple and clear
cons: I didn't find the option to customize the colors and design
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Useful tool for time managment
description: Overall, my experience using My Hours has been good so far. And I have found it to be a valuable tool.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is really easy to set up. Day to day it works great. It shows percentages of where I spend time which is really nice. Also, the customer service is very helpful and timely.
cons: I have not found anything that I do not like about My Hours so far.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: MyHours for the Time Tracking Win!
description: Overall, my team has appreciated the move to MyHours. They are able to track their time on the fly from the mobile app or from their desktops. Knowing we are accurately charging for our time is critical to a business that trades time for money!
author: Joni J.
pros: Ease of use is paramount when choosing a new system in my business. When it came time to track our time more consistently My Hours was an easy choice. The ease of implementation and use has made the transition to formal time tracking a breeze!
cons: Setting up your Projects and Tasks is the foundation of MyHours. Having a bit more training/education on the importance of setting these up at the start would be appreciated.

review_title: Flexible and reliable time tracking for multiple projects
description: Overall I find it easy to log in to MyHours at the start of my work day and to jump between tasks. I rely on the reporting to complete my company monthly timesheet which insists on me entering a % of my time spent on each project and task.
author: David G.
pros: - Easy to start and stop time recording - Ability to continue tracking time of tasks from earlier in the day or week - ability to report the % of my monthly time spent at both project and task level
cons: - in Dashboard report, no way to display a column for % time for tasks inside each project. (I have requested this feature multiple times but you are ignoring me!) The dashboard bar charts DO show % but the table below listing tasks and projects does not and cannot be customized. My employer needs % time use on each task, and currently I need to manually calculate! - in the latest UI, the 'Start new' button starts recording time against an 'empty' record. I am forced to open it and add details. What I want it to do is exactly the same as the '+ Add time log' button. That is, open the editor for me to enter Project, task, etc, and only start recording time when I click the blue 'Add time log' button. - - in the latest UI, when I '+ Add time log' , I want the editor to close immediately after I click the blue 'Add time log' button, to save space of the screen. (It is unlikely users want to add multiple time logs one after the other.) - in the latest UI, the blue 'Add time log' button (below the editor) should be renamed "Add and Start" to make it obvious that time recording will start. - the 'Favorite log' feature is non-intuitive and clunky. Suggest adding a simple 'star' icon to each task which I can click to make it a favorite. OR on the Edit time log dialog, add a 'star' icon I can click - when I 'Add a favourite time log', I want the editor to close immediately, to save space of the screen. (It is unlikely users want to add multiple favorites one after the other.)

review_title: Easy to use though lacking key features for invoicing
description: Overall, my experience has been good with the exception of the customer service interaction - providing a poor excuse for the lack if functionality - and the extra time required to create, export and then combine Activity Reports into Invoice PDFs.
author: Jesse M.
pros: The app is super easy to setup, launch and provides options for various billing rates and billing increments (on the paid plans). The interface is fairly intuitive and nicely laid out.
cons: There is currently no way to show the details of time billed, when creating invoices. So, you can create an invoice that shows one line item and a total.. which is a huge oversight. Most clients require greater detail of time billed - eg what tasks were completed or milestones met - during a billing cycle. Showing specific activity, for hours being billed, is of prime importance to paying customers. The only alternative is to create a separate Activity Report AND an Invoice, and attach or combined the Activity Report to the Invoice PDF. The response I received from Customer Support was that 'My Hours is not an accounting application. It is a top time tracking application that offers simple invoicing.' A poor excuse to say the least. If you are going to include a feature that allows the tracking of details for time billed, allow an option to include that, or not, in the invoice. It really isn't that difficult - I say this as an owner, and long time professional, of a design and development group.

review_title: Happy customer!
description: I use My Hours daily and it helped me a lot to gauge time spent on different projects. It helped me a lot to identify projects that are not profitable. You can easily spend too much time on some projects and My Hours showed me when this happens. It is also ideal to keep track of billable hours for consulting work and I sent My Hours reports to customers to show how time was spent.
author: Timo S.
pros: Having an online time tracking solution helps a lot. Then I know my time tracking is always accessible. My Hours has a very user-friendly interface and provides concepts of tasks and projects. Reporting is very useful and comprehensive. Creating reports for a duration of several years also doesn't take and the interface is very responsive.
cons: I am not sure if there is anything I dislike.

review_title: Time tracking for dummies
description: Generally I am pleased with My Hours and it does what it says on the tin. It is important that I can keep accurate time and associated costs as a draughtsman and particualry for amendments and changes in projects. I also offer software support to my clients and it is vital that I can allocate time against their support contract. In both cases it is important that I can generate a report of usage for my clients - My Hours does it all.
author: Nial B.
pros: I needed a solution for time tracking that was stand alone and would not take me weeks to get to grips with. I was able to set up and use My Hours straight out of the gate.
cons: Initially it took me a little while to get to grips with filtering for reports but it was never a critical failing.

review_title: My Hours Highly Recommended for Time Tracking and Invoicing!
description: I've been using My Hours for over 3 years. Anytime I've had issues, questions or requests, I've been able to chat with the Help staff and ALWAYS receive a prompt response and resolution to my question/issue. I find it to be intuitive and very user friendly - and creates administrative efficiencies so that I can focus on the actual work! Great value for the price!
author: Cathi S.
pros: I am an independent consultant and use My Hours daily to track my work time for several clients. I am able to create tasks specific to my work/industry and thus can track HOW I spend my time for each client. Additionally, I can easily and efficiently generate invoices at the end of each month and attach detailed reports to detail my work.
cons: I'd like to be able to generate an invoice with the corresponding detail/report in one go. Currently, I generate the detailed or dashboard report separately, PDF it and attach it as back-up to the corresponding invoice.

review_title: Great product for contractors
description: I used this as a free product for several years. That is something that My Hours makes possible for contractors like me. However, last year they ran a good deal and I decided the product was worth paying for. It would be good if there was a tiered pricing system so that sole contractors purchase the product for much less than a larger company that uses all the features.
author: Vicke A.
pros: As a sole-proprietor contractor who has a fixed fee per job rather than per hour, I do not use a tenth of the capability of this product. However, part of my license requirements is to keep a log of my time by assignment. This product makes that easy. I can set up multiple projects with multiple clients in just a few minutes. And managing my time recording for multiple projects a day is simple. I'd also like to commend this product for continuous advancements in capability and ease of use.
cons: There are a few things that I either don't understand or don't work exactly like I think they will. For example, I would like my preplanned task list to show up in a specific order when I set up a new project, but I couldn't figure out how to get them in the order I wanted them. I should just contact [SENSITIVE CONTENT], because the few times I've needed help she has always managed to get me sorted out.

review_title: Excellent Tool That Gets The Job Done
description: MyHours does with it sets it out to do and does it very well. Definitely worth every penny (if the free version does not meet your needs).
author: Chidi O.
pros: I've been using MyHours for several years now (initially free but now paid). I've gone through various UI and feature updates but it has remained a robust, intuitive, and very useful tool. I love the reporting features the most as they give me the overview I need to accurately bill my clients. The customer support is also very strong.
cons: Nothing really. I've even had my feature requests implemented by the team simply by asking!

review_title: I use the free version, and I love it
description: Because I work from home, I could not keep track of how much time I was actually putting in on the job. With constant interruptions from children, pets, and spouse, I felt that I was working 8 hours, but I was only getting about 2 hours work done. With this program, I was able to give my family a visual report about how much time I was actually getting to work at one time. Once they understood how their interruptions were interfering with my workday, they were more understanding of my time and started backing off during work hours.
author: Veronica T.
pros: I can track my hours according to client, project, and task. I can create reports based on days, weeks, or months or based on client, project, or task. If I need a report that I cannot figure out how to create, I can download all the data to my hard drive and fiddle with it in Excel. I love this software because I can do almost everything I need to do with a keyboard shortcut. My favorite thing, though, is the ease with which it tracks my hours and lets me know how much time I spent actually working today.
cons: Some of the reports are difficult to figure out how to create, though I do usually get there eventually. Also, I don't have a way to sort according to the Notes, so I had to create separate tasks for each chapter, so I'd know how much time I was spending on specific chapters.

review_title: Amazing for Project Managers & Virtual Assistants
author: Brandi P.
pros: I love the ease of use and how it allows me to manage and keep track of time working on various projects and tasks for many different clients. This software is ESSENTIAL in keeping my business running in an organized way and allowing me to bill people with ease.
cons: I wish I could customize the invoices and add branding to them so they were more personalized to my brand. I have actually considered leaving myhours for other programs that allow me to do that because branding is a huge part of my business.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Some very good bits & 1 or 2 niggles
description: Our old time recording system ceased to be supported so we had to find a new one. We looked at lots of alternatives and ran a new one for almost a year but it was dreadful. Lots of systems want to manage your whole practice rather than just log time so finding My Hours was a real relief. We run it alongside Xero and our CRM both of which we didn’t want to change. Apart from niggles as above this has been a great system for us.
author: Melanie L.
pros: It’s great to be able to log time as you work, whether in the office or in the move. I’m sure being easy to do so means we capture more chargeable hours than we did using our old (very annoying) system.
cons: Overall this is a great product but I have a few niggles which I hope might get sorted out. I don’t like that time is logged to the second rather than whole minutes or nearest 5 minutes - it makes us look money grabbing. Not being able to set up a new chargeable project without having to go into a more detailed screen is annoying and no longer having a link back to the time record from a time report is slowing us down. I don’t like the time report layout as you have to look for the client name and project name - our old original system included a header with this detail which made for better client presentation.
